Guideline: Use Composition API for new components
Description: Composition API provides better TypeScript support and code organization
Do: Use setup script for all new components
Don't: Use Options API for complex components

Guideline: Use script setup for cleaner syntax
Description: Script setup reduces boilerplate and improves performance
Do: Add setup attribute to script tag
Don't: Use setup() function with explicit returns

Guideline: Organize code by feature not option
Description: Group related code together instead of separating by type
Do: Keep state computed and methods for feature together
Don't: Separate all refs from all computed from all functions
Code Good: const count = ref(0); const double = computed(() => count.value * 2); function increment() { count.value++ }
Code Bad: const count = ref(0); const name = ref(''); /* all refs then all computed then all functions */

Description: Create composable functions for shared stateful logic
Do: Create useMouse useLocalStorage composables
Code Good: export function useCounter() { const count = ref(0); return { count } }

Description: ref wraps primitives and provides .value access
Do: Use ref for strings numbers booleans
Code Bad: const state = reactive({ count: 0 }) // overkill for single value

Description: reactive provides deep reactivity without .value
Do: Use reactive for complex nested objects
Don't: Use ref for objects requiring .value everywhere
Code Bad: const state = ref({ user: { name: '' } }); state.value.user.name

Description: Destructuring breaks reactivity connection
Do: Use toRefs when destructuring is needed

Description: computed values cache and update automatically
Do: Use computed for filtered lists or formatted values

Description: shallowRef avoids deep reactivity overhead
Do: Use shallowRef for large datasets updated wholesale
Don't: Use ref/reactive for large objects with deep tracking
Code Bad: const items = ref(largeArray) // tracks every nested change

Description: Use defineProps with type annotation for type safety
Do: Use generic defineProps<T>() syntax

Description: Use withDefaults for complex default values
Do: Use withDefaults helper for object/array defaults

Description: Use validators for runtime prop validation
Do: Add validator function for complex requirements
Code Bad: defineProps({ status: String }) // accepts any string

Description: Never mutate props directly in child components
Do: Emit events to update parent state

Description: Declare emitted events for documentation and validation
Do: Use defineEmits with type annotations
Code Good: const emit = defineEmits<{ (e: 'update', id: number): void }>()

Description: v-model simplifies parent-child state sync
Do: Use v-model with update:modelValue emit

Description: Use descriptive event names following conventions
Do: Use verb:noun pattern like update:value submit:form
Don't: Use generic names like change or event

Description: Access DOM elements after component mounts
Do: Use onMounted for DOM measurements and third-party libraries
Don't: Access DOM in setup before mount
Code Bad: // In setup: ref.value.focus() // ref is null

Description: Remove event listeners and cancel subscriptions
Do: Return cleanup in onMounted or use onUnmounted
Code Good: onMounted(() => { window.addEventListener('resize', handler); onUnmounted(() => window.removeEventListener('resize', handler)) })

Description: watchEffect runs immediately and tracks dependencies automatically
Do: Use watchEffect for effects that should run immediately

Description: watch is explicit about what triggers the effect
Do: Use watch when you need old and new values
Don't: Use watchEffect when you need specific triggers
Code Bad: watchEffect(() => { /* but I only care about count */ })

Description: SFCs keep template script and style together
Do: Create .vue files with all three sections
Don't: Split template script style into separate files

Description: Each component should have single responsibility
Do: Extract reusable parts into child components
Don't: Create large components with many responsibilities

Guideline: Use async components for code splitting
Description: Load components on demand to reduce bundle size
Do: Use defineAsyncComponent for heavy components

Description: Use slots for flexible component composition
Do: Define default slot content

Description: Convention makes composable functions identifiable
Do: Name all composables useXxx

Description: Return refs for consistent .value usage
Do: Return object of refs from composables
Don't: Return reactive object that loses reactivity when destructured
Code Bad: return reactive({ count, increment }) // loses reactivity on destructure

Description: Clean up side effects when component unmounts
Do: Use onUnmounted inside composables for cleanup

Description: Make composables flexible by accepting refs or values
Do: Use MaybeRef type and unref helper
Code Good: function useFeature(maybeRef: MaybeRef<string>) { const value = unref(maybeRef) }

Description: v-if completely removes elements from DOM
Do: Use v-if for expensive components or rare conditions

Description: v-show uses CSS display and is faster for frequent changes
Do: Use v-show for elements toggled often
Don't: Use v-if for frequently toggled elements

Description: Keys help Vue track list items efficiently
Do: Use unique stable IDs as keys
Don't: Use array index as key for dynamic lists

Description: v-if has higher priority causing unexpected behavior
Do: Use computed to filter list before v-for
Don't: Put v-if on same element as v-for
Code Good: const filtered = computed(() => items.filter(...))<li v-for='item in filtered'>

Description: Pinia is the recommended state management for Vue 3
Do: Use Pinia stores for shared state
Don't: Use Vuex or provide/inject for global state

Description: Use setup stores for better TypeScript support
Do: Define store using setup function
Code Good: defineStore('counter', () => { const count = ref(0); return { count } })

Description: storeToRefs maintains reactivity when destructuring
Do: Use storeToRefs for state and getters
Code Bad: const { count } = store // count is not reactive

Description: Access route params with useRoute composable
Do: Use useRoute().params reactively
Code Good: const route = useRoute(); const id = computed(() => route.params.id)

Description: Navigate programmatically with useRouter
Do: Use router.push for navigation

Description: Use navigation guards for auth and permissions
Do: Use beforeEach for global auth checks
Code Bad: // Check auth in each page component

Description: v-once renders element once and skips future updates
Do: Use v-once for truly static content

Description: v-memo caches template based on dependency array
Do: Use v-memo for list items with expensive templates

Description: KeepAlive preserves component state when switching
Do: Use KeepAlive for tabs or route components
Code Bad: // Component remounts on every tab switch

Description: defineComponent enables proper TypeScript support
Do: Wrap components in defineComponent

Description: Use ref<HTMLElement | null> for template refs
Do: Type refs with specific element type
Don't: Use untyped refs for template elements

Description: Define complex prop types with PropType helper
Do: Use PropType for object and function props
Code Good: defineProps({ user: { type: Object as PropType<User> } })

Guideline: Use Vue Test Utils for component tests
Description: Official testing library for Vue components
Do: Use mount or shallowMount for component tests
Don't: Test components with generic DOM testing

Description: Write tests that resemble user interactions
Do: Test rendered output and user interactions
Don't: Test internal data or methods directly

Description: v-model provides two-way binding for form fields
Do: Use v-model on input select textarea
Don't: Manually bind value and handle input

Description: Prefer native HTML elements over divs with ARIA
Do: Use button for clickable actions
Don't: Use div with click handler for buttons

Description: Nuxt provides SSR hydration and routing out of the box
Do: Use Nuxt for SSR/SSG Vue applications
Don't: Build custom SSR setup from scratch
